review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to boundary protection. However, some naming improvements are suggested for better clarity and consistency with Kubernetes terminology. The function names are mostly descriptive and specific, indicating clear pass/fail conditions.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'k8s_networkpolicy_restrict_public_ip_access' to 'k8s_networkpolicy_public_ip_access_restricted' for consistency with naming patterns.
title: Boundary Protection - Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible...
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7-b focuses on boundary protection by implementing subnetworks for publicly accessible resources. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to network security controls that restrict public access to cluster resources. NetworkPolicies can be used to enforce default deny ingress rules, ensuring that only explicitly allowed traffic can reach pods. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer services to prevent exposing services directly to the internet aligns with the control's intent. Enabling TLS for Ingress resources further secures public endpoints.
